exclusion: Law-firm commentary used as primary regulatory interpretation (cite the underlying regulation, not the law firm).
Opinion pieces in general-interest media unless written by a named policy analyst with disclosed affiliation.
Press releases from pharmaceutical manufacturers without a regulatory decision document to corroborate.
inclusion: Government press releases and public statements.
HTA agency reports (ICER, NICE, CADTH, IQWiG, HAS, AHRQ).
NGO reports with traceable funding disclosure.
Peer-reviewed health policy / health economics journal articles.
Regulatory documents from FDA, EMA, Health Canada, MHRA, TGA, PMDA, NMPA.

expected_tier_distribution.rationale: Regulatory documents and HTA reports are the primary evidence base for policy research.
Peer-reviewed primary studies (health economics, epidemiology) supporting the policy claims.
Systematic reviews and HTA-style evidence synthesis.
Policy commentary from named analysts and NGO reports. Treat as interpretive layer on top of primary regulatory documents.
Narrative reviews of the policy landscape.
Industry policy pages — allowed when corroborated by T3.
Conference abstracts — rarely relevant for policy research.

research_question: How is the FDA regulating AI-enabled medical devices under the current Predetermined Change Control Plan framework?
